title: Sex- and race-based disparities in in-hospital outcomes among U.S. hospitalizations for lupus nephritis: a National Inpatient Sample analysis
text:
Study Objective: To evaluate the burden and outcomes of lupus nephritis hospitalization and disparities in in-hospital outcomes by sex and by race.
Data Source: Cross-sectional analysis of the National Inpatient Sample (NIS) from 2017 to 2022. The NIS is part of the Healthcare Cost and Utilization Project (HCUP) and provides a stratified sample of inpatient discharges from U.S. community hospitals across all payers.
Inclusion Criteria:

Age в‰Ґ 18 years
Diagnosis of lupus nephritis in any diagnoses fields: M32.14 and M32.15

Disparities:

Sex categories: Male and female
Race categories: White, Black, Hispanic, Asian or Pacific Islander, Native American, and other

Primary Outcomes:

In-hospital all-cause mortality
Total hospital length of stay (days)
Total charge, inflation adjusted to 2021 ($)

Secondary Outcomes:

Mechanical Ventilation
Renal Replacement Therapy
Acute kidney injury
Non-home discharge

Covariates for Adjustment: All models adjusted for the following:

Patient Characteristics:: Age, sex, race, income, insurance,
Hospital Characteristics: hospital region, hospital bedsize, hospital location and teaching status, day and type of admission.
Comorbidities: Chronic heart failure, diabetes mellitus, hypertension, chronic kidney disease, obesity, coronary artery disease, hyperlipidemia, anemia, chronic pulmonary disease, and antiphospholipid syndrome.

Statistical Methods:

Univariable Analysis:

Categorical variables: Compared using PearsonвЂ™s chi-square test with Rao & Scott adjustment
Continuous variables: Analyzed using the design-based KruskalвЂ“Wallis test, a non-parametric alternative suitable for survey-weighted data

Multivariable Analysis:

Binary outcomes (e.g., mortality): Survey-weighted logistic regression using the quasibinomial family
Continuous outcomes (e.g., LOS, hospital charges): Survey-weighted linear regression using the gaussian family
Results reported as adjusted odds ratios (aORs) or beta coefficients with 95% confidence intervals


Software: All statistical analyses were performed using the R Statistical Language (Version 4.5.0; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria), incorporating survey-weighted procedures via the survey package to account for the complex sampling design of NIS.

Continuous outcomes (e.g., LOS, hospital charges): Survey-weighted linear regression using the gaussian family

Software: All statistical analyses were performed using the R Statistical Language (Version 4.5.0; R Foundation for Statistical Computing, Vienna, Austria), incorporating survey-weighted procedures via the survey package to account for the complex sampling design of NIS.
